workItem.title: Checkpoint long Codex turns before token growth becomes wasteful

## Context

Autonomous delivery of Symphony issues 6 through 9 consumed approximately 1.56M, 0.41M, 4.51M and 3.79M total input tokens respectively. Cache reuse was high, but issues 8 and 9 continued streaming after the implementation diff and gates were effectively stable, and neither produced a commit before operator takeover.

## Expected outcome

Symphony enforces configurable elapsed-time and uncached-input budgets per Codex role, requests a durable checkpoint before exhaustion, stops cleanly, and resumes from the same AgentRun without discarding useful work or launching blind retries.

## Scope

- Add role-specific wall-clock and uncached-input soft and hard budgets
- Use uncached input rather than aggregate cached input for cost decisions
- At the soft limit request a concise checkpoint and completion or handoff
- At the hard limit stop the App Server turn while preserving workspace and outbox durability
- Expose budget reason and consumed totals in runtime status and AuditEvents
- Resume the same Attempt when safe and prevent an identical over-budget retry loop
- Stage validation so expensive full gates run only after targeted checks and stable diff

## Out of scope

- Dropping active work without a checkpoint
- Using cached-token totals as the enforcement metric
- Weakening required quality gates

## Acceptance criteria

- A synthetic over-budget turn checkpoints and stops within the configured bound
- The same AgentRun resumes without duplicate PR, Evidence or Review artifacts
- Status distinguishes soft-limit, hard-limit and provider-rate-limit pauses
- A stable implementation does not continue consuming tokens after all required gates complete
- The full Symphony gate passes with 100 percent coverage and zero Dialyzer findings
